explicit_text: I was born in Monrovia, the capital city of Liberia, on March 23, 1994. Monrovia is where I spent my early childhood before the war forced my family to make difficult decisions. It’s a warm place with a rainy season and a dry season, very different from the cold weather I later experienced. My uncle took care of me in Monrovia when my mom moved to the United States to find a better life.

explicit_text: My mom moved to the United States after the war in Liberia to find a better life, but I only joined her years later in 2013. When I traveled to the United States, I experienced cold weather for the first time, especially when we arrived in Chicago and later Minnesota. The United States is very different from Liberia, especially in terms of climate, with snow and freezing temperatures that I wasn’t used to. My mom gave me a colorful jacket that kept me warm during my first winter here, and I still use it every year.

explicit_text: After leaving Chicago, we went to Minnesota where the weather was even colder, around 20 degrees. I was freezing and kept my jacket on the whole time, even through the night and the next day. Minnesota’s cold winters were a big adjustment for me coming from Liberia’s warm climate. That jacket my mom gave me has been a lifesaver here, keeping me warm and healthy during the harsh winter months.
